title: Mona Lisa
insight: The Mona Lisa was stolen from the Louvre in 1911, and that theft helped make it world-famous.

title: The Last Supper
insight: Leonardo used an experimental technique here, and it began deteriorating surprisingly quickly.

title: The Birth of Venus
insight: This was one of the first large-scale mythological paintings made in Renaissance Florence.

title: Primavera
insight: The painting is packed with botanical detail—scholars have identified hundreds of plant species in it.

title: The Creation of Adam
insight: Many scholars think the shape around God resembles a human brain, adding a layer of symbolic meaning.

title: David
insight: Michelangelo carved David from a huge block of marble that other sculptors had already rejected.

title: The School of Athens
insight: Raphael painted a gathering of ancient philosophers and is believed to have included his own likeness in the fresco.

title: Sistine Madonna
insight: The two cherubs at the bottom became so famous that they took on a cultural life far beyond the painting itself.

title: Venus of Urbino
insight: Venus of Urbino is a strong example of renaissance art, and its lasting appeal comes from how clearly it turns style into atmosphere.

title: Assumption of the Virgin
insight: Assumption of the Virgin reflects how artists used familiar sacred stories to experiment with drama, composition, and emotion.

title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
insight: The sitter's identity is still uncertain, which is part of why the painting feels so mysterious.

title: The Night Watch
insight: The painting looks darker today than it originally did because old varnish changed its appearance over time.

title: Self-Portrait with Two Circles
insight: Self-Portrait with Two Circles invites viewers to read expression, pose, and status closely, which is one reason portrait works stay so psychologically compelling.

title: Las Meninas
insight: The painting plays with perspective so cleverly that viewers still debate who exactly is being portrayed and from where.
